title: IndexNow Key Installation
subtitle: Add your IndexNow key to enable instant search engine indexing
autoDeployFailed: Automatic deployment was not possible. Follow these steps to add the key manually.

prereq.fileManagerOrFtp: File Manager plugin or FTP access

step1.title: Log in to WordPress Admin
step1.description: Go to your WordPress dashboard at yourdomain.com/wp-admin and log in with your admin credentials.

step2.title: Open File Manager
step2.description: Install and open the 'File Manager' plugin from Plugins > Add New. Search for 'WP File Manager' and activate it.
step2.tip: If you already have File Manager installed, skip the installation and go directly to WP File Manager in your sidebar.

step3.title: Navigate to Root Directory
step3.description: In File Manager, navigate to the root directory of your WordPress installation. This is usually 'public_html' or 'www' folder, where you see wp-content, wp-admin, etc.

step4.title: Create the Key File
step4.description: Click 'Create New File' and enter the filename below. Then paste the API key as the file content.

step5.title: Save and Verify
step5.description: Save the file. The filename must exactly match the content (your API key).
step5.warning: Make sure there are no extra spaces or line breaks in the file content.

step1.title: Log in to Shopify Admin
step1.description: Go to your Shopify admin panel at yourdomain.myshopify.com/admin

step2.title: Go to Content > Files
step2.description: In the left sidebar, click 'Content' then 'Files'. This is where Shopify stores static files.

step3.title: Upload the Key File
step3.description: Create a text file on your computer with the API key as content, name it with the key + .txt extension, then upload it here.
step3.tip: Shopify may not serve .txt files directly from the Files section. If this doesn't work, use the theme.liquid method in step 4.

step4.title: Alternative: Edit theme.liquid
step4.description: Go to Online Store > Themes > Actions > Edit code. Open 'Layout/theme.liquid' and add this code just after the opening <body> tag:
step4.warning: Be careful when editing theme files. Make a backup first.

step1.title: Open Wix Dashboard
step1.description: Log in to your Wix account and open your site's dashboard.

step2.title: Go to Settings
step2.description: Click 'Settings' in the left sidebar, then find 'Custom Code' or 'Advanced Settings'.

step3.title: Enable Velo (Dev Mode)
step3.description: Turn on Velo by clicking 'Dev Mode' in the top menu. This enables custom code.
step3.tip: Velo requires a Premium plan. If you don't have one, contact us for alternative solutions.

step4.title: Add Router Code
step4.description: In the Velo sidebar, create or open routers.js and add the following code:
step4.warning: This requires familiarity with Wix Velo. If unsure, contact your developer.

step1.title: Open Squarespace Settings
step1.description: Log in to Squarespace and go to your site's Settings panel.
step1.warning: Squarespace has limited support for serving raw .txt files. You may need to use an alternative method.

step2.title: Go to Advanced Settings
step2.description: Navigate to Settings > Advanced > Code Injection.

step3.title: Add Code Injection
step3.description: Squarespace doesn't directly support serving .txt files. Consider using one of these alternatives:
step3.tip: The easiest option is to use a URL redirect service or host the file elsewhere.

step4.title: Alternative Methods
step4.description: Option 1: Create a page with URL /{apiKey} and add the key as plain text content. Option 2: Use a redirect to an externally hosted file.

step5.title: Consider External Hosting
step5.description: For reliable IndexNow support, consider hosting the key file on a CDN like Cloudflare R2 or AWS S3, then redirecting to it.

step1.title: Open Webflow Designer
step1.description: Log in to Webflow and open your project in the Designer.

step2.title: Go to Assets Panel
step2.description: Click the Assets panel (folder icon) in the left sidebar.

step3.title: Upload Key File
step3.description: Create a .txt file with your API key on your computer, then drag and drop it into the Assets panel. Note the asset URL after upload.
step3.tip: Webflow serves assets from a CDN with a different URL. You may need to set up a redirect.

step4.title: Publish Changes
step4.description: Click 'Publish' in the top right to deploy your changes to the live site.

step1.title: Open Your Project
step1.description: Access your project's source code in your local development environment or GitHub.

step2.title: Add to Public Folder
step2.description: Create a new file in your 'public' folder (or 'static' for some frameworks) with the key filename and content.

step3.title: Alternative: Use vercel.json
step3.description: If the public folder approach doesn't work, you can create an API route and use rewrites in vercel.json.
step3.tip: The public folder method is simpler and works for most frameworks (Next.js, Create React App, etc.)

step4.title: Deploy
step4.description: Commit and push your changes. Vercel will automatically deploy the update.

step1.title: Open Your Project
step1.description: Access your project's source code locally or in your Git repository.

step2.title: Add to Public/Static Folder
step2.description: Create the key file in your 'public' or 'static' folder depending on your framework.

step3.title: Alternative: Use _redirects
step3.description: Create a _redirects file in your publish directory to handle the route.
step3.tip: Make sure the _redirects file is in your build output directory, not just the source.

step4.title: Deploy
step4.description: Push your changes to trigger a new Netlify build and deployment.

step1.title: Log in to cPanel
step1.description: Access your hosting control panel, usually at yourdomain.com/cpanel or yourdomain.com:2083

step2.title: Open File Manager
step2.description: In the Files section, click on 'File Manager' to open the web-based file browser.

step3.title: Navigate to public_html
step3.description: Click on 'public_html' (or 'www' or 'htdocs' depending on your host). This is your website's root directory.
step3.tip: If you have multiple domains, make sure you're in the correct domain's folder.

step4.title: Create New File
step4.description: Click '+ File' in the top menu to create a new file. Enter the filename exactly as shown:

step5.title: Edit File Content
step5.description: Right-click the new file and select 'Edit' or 'Code Edit'. Paste only the API key (no extra characters) and save.
step5.warning: Ensure there are no blank lines or spaces before or after the key.

step1.title: Download an FTP Client
step1.description: Download and install FileZilla (free) or use your preferred FTP client.

step2.title: Connect to Your Server
step2.description: Open FileZilla and enter your FTP credentials: Host (usually ftp.yourdomain.com), Username, Password, and Port (usually 21 for FTP or 22 for SFTP).
step2.tip: Check your hosting welcome email or control panel for FTP credentials.

step3.title: Navigate to Web Root
step3.description: In the remote site panel (right side), navigate to your website's root directory. Common paths: /public_html, /www, /htdocs, or /html

step4.title: Create the Key File Locally
step4.description: On your computer, create a new text file with the exact filename and content shown below.

step5.title: Upload the File
step5.description: Drag the file from your local site (left panel) to the remote site (right panel) in the root directory.
step5.warning: Make sure you're uploading to the root directory, not a subdirectory.

verify.title: Verify Installation
verify.instruction: After completing the steps above, verify the installation:
verify.visitUrl: Visit this URL in your browser:
verify.expectedContent: You should see only the API key with no other content.
verify.successMessage: Your IndexNow key is properly installed. Search engines can now instantly discover your content updates.
verify.failedMessage: We couldn't find your key file. Please check the common errors below.

404.title: File Not Found (404)
404.description: The key file doesn't exist at the expected URL.
404.solution: Double-check the filename matches exactly (case-sensitive) and the file is in the root directory.

wrongContent.title: Wrong File Content
wrongContent.description: The file exists but contains incorrect content.
wrongContent.solution: Open the file and ensure it contains ONLY the API key - no extra spaces, line breaks, or HTML.

htaccess.title: .htaccess Blocking
htaccess.description: WordPress security plugins or .htaccess rules may be blocking .txt files.
htaccess.solution: Check your .htaccess file for rules blocking .txt files, or whitelist this specific file in your security plugin.

shopifyRedirect.title: Shopify Redirect Issue
shopifyRedirect.description: Shopify is redirecting the .txt file request.
shopifyRedirect.solution: Use the theme.liquid method instead of the Files upload method.

liquidSyntax.title: Liquid Syntax Error
liquidSyntax.description: The Liquid code has a syntax error.
liquidSyntax.solution: Check for missing quotes, brackets, or typos in the Liquid code. Make sure you copied all of it.

wixPremium.title: Wix Premium Required
wixPremium.description: Wix Velo requires a Premium plan.
wixPremium.solution: Upgrade to Wix Premium or contact us for alternative deployment methods.

wixVelo.title: Velo Not Enabled
wixVelo.description: Wix Velo (Dev Mode) is not enabled.
wixVelo.solution: Enable Dev Mode from the top menu bar in the Wix Editor.

squarespaceNoTxt.title: Squarespace .txt Limitation
squarespaceNoTxt.description: Squarespace doesn't natively support serving .txt files.
squarespaceNoTxt.solution: Use the external hosting workaround or contact us for help.

squarespacePlan.title: Plan Limitation
squarespacePlan.description: Code injection requires Squarespace Business plan or higher.
squarespacePlan.solution: Upgrade your Squarespace plan or use an external file hosting solution.

webflowAsset.title: Asset URL Mismatch
webflowAsset.description: Webflow serves assets from a CDN with a different URL.
webflowAsset.solution: Set up a redirect from your domain to the Webflow asset URL, or use a custom domain for assets.

webflowPublish.title: Changes Not Published
webflowPublish.description: Changes haven't been published to the live site.
webflowPublish.solution: Click 'Publish' in the Webflow Designer and ensure you're publishing to the correct domain.

vercelPublic.title: File Not in Public Folder
vercelPublic.description: The file isn't being served because it's not in the correct folder.
vercelPublic.solution: Ensure the file is in 'public/' (not 'src/public/' or another location). Check your framework's docs for the correct static file directory.

vercelCache.title: Vercel Cache Issue
vercelCache.description: Vercel's CDN may be serving a cached version.
vercelCache.solution: Go to your Vercel dashboard, find the deployment, and click 'Redeploy' with the cache cleared.

netlifyPath.title: Wrong Directory
netlifyPath.description: The file isn't in the published directory.
netlifyPath.solution: Check your netlify.toml or build settings for the publish directory. The file must be in the final build output.

netlifyBuild.title: Build Not Triggered
netlifyBuild.description: The deploy didn't run or failed.
netlifyBuild.solution: Check the Netlify dashboard for deploy status. Trigger a manual deploy if needed.

cpanelPath.title: Wrong Directory
cpanelPath.description: The file is not in the website's root directory.
cpanelPath.solution: Make sure the file is directly in public_html (or your domain's root), not in a subdirectory.

cpanelPermissions.title: File Permissions
cpanelPermissions.solution: Right-click the file, select 'Change Permissions', and set it to 644.

ftpConnection.title: Connection Failed
ftpConnection.description: Cannot connect to the FTP server.
ftpConnection.solution: Verify your credentials, check if your IP is whitelisted, and try both FTP (port 21) and SFTP (port 22).

ftpDirectory.title: Wrong Directory
ftpDirectory.solution: Navigate to the correct root directory. Common paths: /public_html, /www, /htdocs. Look for where index.html or index.php exists.

ftpPermissions.title: Upload Permission Denied
ftpPermissions.description: You don't have permission to upload to this directory.
ftpPermissions.solution: Contact your hosting provider to ensure your FTP account has write permissions to the web root.
